spans.text: holy fortitude , The path of good right
He , to whose eternal throne on high The sufferers of the earth with anguish cry , Be thy protector
thee patient to the last abode Of wretchedness , in peril and in pain , May He thy steps direct , thy heart sustain
He thy steps direct , thy heart sustain
To cheer the dismal wastes of human woe
the dismal wastes of human woe
OH Charity ! our helpless nature's pride
fragrance fresh his weary frame , Aught like the incense of thy sacred flame
his weary frame , Aught like the incense of thy sacred flame
like saddest moonlight on the hill And distant grove , when the wide world is still
saddest moonlight on the hill And distant grove , when the wide world is still
moonlight on the hill And distant grove , when the wide world is still
the prison's gloom , Where ghastly Guilt implores her lingering doom
the prison's gloom , Where ghastly Guilt implores her lingering doom
Where Agony , half-famished , cries in vain ; Where dark Despondence murmurs over her chain ; Where gaunt Disease is wasted to the bone , And hollow-eyed Despair forgets to groan
That never told to human ears her tale
Where Agony , half-famished , cries in vain ; Where dark Despondence murmurs over her chain ; Where gaunt Disease is wasted to the bone , And hollow-eyed Despair forgets to groan
Where dark Despondence murmurs over her chain
In horror's realm the voice of peace
In horror's realm the voice of peace
full many a livid mien , And haggard eye-ball , through the dusk are seen
a livid mien , And haggard eye-ball , through the dusk
for ever closed the jealous door
my bed on the forsaken floor
Howard what man owes to man
Dragging a droning flight from flower to flower
assigned In the sad fellowship of human kind
the bitter pain Of a poor brother
should bestow An unavailing tear on want or woe
That Comfort cheers the dark abode of pain
the labouring breast , While long-forgotten tears of virtue flow ; Thou , generous friend of all — to thee we owe
her views expand To many a cheerless haunt , and distant land
how vain The triumphs of weak man
how vain The triumphs of weak man
to Conquest's crimson car , Amid the bannered host
before him flies Affright , on pinions fleeter than the wind
The havoc of his echoing march
the loud clarion of his fame One day
The holy deeds of Mercy and of Love
the vanities of life's brief day Oblivion's hurrying wing
